title: NOC 73110 – Roofers and shinglers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73110 professionals are the primary protectors of Canada’s residential, commercial, and industrial infrastructure. As a TEER 3 occupation (recognized as a skilled trade), Roofers and Shinglers install, repair, and replace a vast array of roofing systems designed to withstand Canada’s extreme climate. In 2026, as the federal government targets 5.8 million new homes by 2033, these workers are classified as Critical Construction Talent. For immigration, this role is a top-tier candidate for the 2026 Trade-Specific Express Entry draws and the New 2026 Construction Worker PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group731 – Masonry and plastering tradesMinor Group7311 – Roofers, shinglers and glaziersNOC Code73110

duties: Built-up Systems: Install, repair, or replace built-up roofing using materials such as asphalt-saturated felts, hot asphalt, and gravel.
Single-ply Systems: Install and repair single-ply roofing systems using waterproof sheet materials like modified plastics or elastomeric compositions.
Shingle Installation: Install or replace asphalt shingles, wood shingles, and shakes on sloped residential and commercial roofs.
Masonry Roofing: Fit and secure masonry or baked clay roofing tiles on sloped surfaces according to architectural specifications.
Metal Roofing: Install and repair standing seam or corrugated metal roofs using specialized hand and power tools.
Flashing & Metalwork: Install sheet metal flashings to ensure watertight seals around chimneys, vents, and roof edges.
Waterproofing: Apply specialized waterproof coatings to concrete or other masonry surfaces both above and below ground level.
Scaffolding & Safety: Set up scaffolding and rigging to provide safe access to rooftops; utilize fall-protection systems as per provincial safety regulations.
Green & Solar: Install "living" green roofs and integrated solar mounting systems as part of sustainable building projects.
Cost Estimation: Inspect existing roof structures to identify leaks and damage; estimate required materials and provide formal cost quotes for clients.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a two-to-three-year apprenticeship program is the standard path to qualification.
Experience: Alternatively, over three years of work experience in the trade and some industry-certified courses are usually required to be eligible for trade certification.
Trade Certification:Compulsory in Quebec.Available but voluntary in all other provinces and territories.Residential Steep Roofer certification is available and voluntary in British Columbia.
Available but voluntary in all other provinces and territories.
Residential Steep Roofer certification is available and voluntary in British Columbia.
Red Seal Endorsement: Highly recommended; it allows for interprovincial mobility and is a significant advantage in the Federal Skilled Trades Program.
Safety Training: Completion of a Working at Heights training program is mandatory in most provinces.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, over 77% of workers in this NOC receive at least one type of non-wage benefit, such as dental, medical, or employer-sponsored pension plans. Experienced foremen or specialized metal roofers often earn annual salaries exceeding $90,000.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. With nearly 50% of new jobs in the coming years resulting from the need to replace retiring workers, the "Replacement Demand" is critical. Additionally, the shift toward climate-resilient roofing and energy-efficient retrofits will ensure that Roofers remain the most in-demand trade in the renovation market through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Roofers and Shinglers in 2026 is Strong across almost all provinces. In Ontario and British Columbia, massive infrastructure investments in transit and health care, combined with a persistent housing shortage, have led to a "Moderate to Good" outlook. According to 2026 BuildForce Canada data, the industry must grow its workforce by 83% to meet national housing goals. Because roofs require periodic replacement regardless of new construction trends, this occupation offers recession-proof job security in the maintenance and renovation sectors.
High-Growth Industry: Benefit from a decade-long construction boom driven by aggressive federal housing targets and green-building retrofits.
Evolution of Trade: Work with advanced materials including integrated solar shingles, "cool roof" membranes, and high-performance synthetic shakes.
Top Earnings in Trades: Skilled roofers, especially those with Red Seal certification, command high hourly rates and have significant potential for overtime earnings.
Priority Immigration Status: IRCC has allocated 14,000 spots in 2026 specifically for construction workers, making this one of the fastest routes to Permanent Residency.
pathways: New 2026 Construction Worker PR Pathway: A dedicated allocation of 14,000 spots for foreign construction nationals.
Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Frequent draws targeting NOC 73110 with lower CRS cut-offs compared to the general pool.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): For those with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
Provincial Nominee Programs (PNP): Highly sought after in the BC PNP Skilled Worker and Saskatchewan SINP streams.
